evals.name: cache-key-daily-stability
evals.prompt: I'm building a React wellness app and want to add a daily tarot card + AI oracle message that generates once per day and stays the same if the user refreshes. How do I implement the caching so each user gets their own reading that's stable for the whole day?
evals.expected_output: Agent provides the getOrCreateOracle() function with the cache key pattern `{appPrefix}:{userId}:oracle:{YYYY-MM-DD}`. Uses new Date().toISOString().split('T')[0] for the date string. Explains per-user isolation via userId component.
assertions: Response uses a cache key pattern that includes both a userId component and a YYYY-MM-DD date string (e.g. `myapp:${userId}:oracle:${today}`)
Response uses localStorage.getItem and localStorage.setItem as the storage mechanism
Response extracts the date as new Date().toISOString().split('T')[0] — not toLocaleDateString() or toDateString()
Response explains that the userId component provides per-user isolation so different users get different readings on the same day

evals.name: tarot-api-fallback
evals.prompt: My daily oracle app fetches a tarot card from tarotapi.dev. What happens if that API goes down? I don't want the user to see a blank card or an error — I want a graceful fallback. Walk me through the exact fallback strategy.
evals.expected_output: Agent describes the deterministic day-of-year fallback used by this repo: the 22-card MAJOR_ARCANA pool selected by dayOfYear % MAJOR_ARCANA.length. Explains the day-of-year calculation. Confirms user always sees a meaningful reading.
assertions: Response describes a deterministic fallback — not a random selection and not a static single card
Response references the repo's fixed 22-card MAJOR_ARCANA fallback pool
Response includes the day-of-year calculation using Date.now() and getTime() subtraction divided by 86400000
Response uses modulo (`doy % FALLBACK.length` or equivalent) to select from the pool, ensuring determinism across the year

evals.name: anthropic-direct-browser
evals.prompt: I want to add Claude-generated oracle messages to my personal app deployed on GitHub Pages. I don't have a backend server. How do I call the Anthropic API directly from the browser in a Vite/React app?
evals.expected_output: Agent provides the direct browser fetch approach with the anthropic-dangerous-direct-browser-access header, using VITE_ANTHROPIC_API_KEY as the env var. Includes bundle exposure warning and personal-use caveat.
assertions: Response includes the `anthropic-dangerous-direct-browser-access: 'true'` request header by exact name
Response uses `import.meta.env.VITE_ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` as the environment variable pattern (Vite-specific)
Response explicitly warns that the API key will be visible in the JavaScript bundle
Response states this approach is only acceptable for personal or single-user apps — not for public-facing multi-user deployments
